description.value:
The dancer's second dance is used as support and cover, often times set up long before the enemy arrives, as the user continues the dance for as long as needed. This technique can only be used while the user is in the Meimu no Kamae stance. The user may make one advanced change per two levels. An advanced change is something of significant importance, such as changing the color of a room or adding a door where there isn't any, and even moving or removing a tree from its current location, as well as making objects of Mediumsize or smaller appear or disappear and such, but it cannot affect a creature or cause harm in any way.

A creature that interacts with an objects created by this technique will believe that it is real and appear to affect it, but in reality no such thing happens (a bridge will feel real, but the creature will fall through if it passes through it, just like a glass of water will appear to be refreshing but have in fact no effect. This technique cannot be disbelieved until it is identified or studied, and can be dispelled. This technique can only affect unattended objects. Until the user stops the dance, the user of this technique gains a +1 Dodge bonus to Defence due to the difficult to predict movements the dance has them perform.

A creature that enters the area, unless the user chooses, cannot see or hear the user's dance, but is still affected by it.

Requires: Taijutsu 8 ranks; Meimu no Kamae (t)

Material Focus: To accurately perform this technique, the user needs specially made clothes, usually equipped with whistles or bells that cause the illusion. These clothes usually have a Cost DC of 10.
